policy_statement: Install Visual Guidance Docking System (VGDS) at airports to reduce aircraft idling.
mechanism_description: Installing visual guidance docking systems at airport gates reduces aircraft idling during docking, cutting fuel use and emissions from ground operations.
primary_causal_pathway: Operational change in aviation ground movements → ↓ aircraft engine idling time / ↓ fuel combustion → ↓ GHG emissions → ↓ atmospheric forcing
causal_mechanism_detail:
(1) Change in activity/system: Airports install and use visual guidance docking systems (and associated procedures) so pilots can align and park at gates faster and more precisely, reducing waiting, misalignment corrections, and engine-on time during docking.  
(2) Mediating physical process: Less jet fuel is combusted by aircraft engines (and potentially reduced auxiliary power demand if ground time is shortened), directly reducing combustion-related emissions.  
(3) Climate-relevant outcome: Lower CO₂ (and small reductions in other climate-relevant pollutants) from airport ground operations.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is reduced aircraft ground fuel burn during docking/taxi-in, leading to lower CO₂ (and other) emissions.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: Technology-enabled operational efficiency reduces aircraft engine idling and fuel combustion on the ground → lowers GHG emissions.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While it also improves operational efficiency and local air quality, the main climate-relevant endpoint is direct emissions reduction from avoided fuel burn, which is Mitigation rather than Adaptation, Resource Efficiency (as a standalone category), or Nature-Based Solutions.  
3) Edge cases applied: If VGDS indirectly increases throughput and could induce additional flights, that rebound is context-specific and not inherent to the mechanism; the mechanism itself primarily reduces per-operation fuel burn/emissions.  
4) Applies to ALL instances: Any policy implementing airport gate docking guidance to reduce idling has the same primary effect—reduced fuel combustion during ground operations—so Mitigation is consistent across instances.  
5) Secondary subtype deferred: Any further subcategory (e.g., aviation operational efficiency, ground operations electrification interactions) requires policy-specific evidence and is not assigned here.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: Potential rebound/induced demand from improved gate efficiency; variability depending on whether reductions come from main-engine idling vs APU use and whether other ground power measures are paired.
key_indicators: "Install Visual Guidance Docking System (VGDS)"; "at airports"; "to reduce aircraft idling"
co_benefits: Reduced local air pollutants (NOx/PM) near airports; reduced noise and operational delays; improved gate safety/efficiency
instance_edge_case_notes: Could also be implemented via procurement, but the text is framed as installation of equipment (capital investment) rather than a purchasing rule or operational mandate.
